skill: Nonlinear equations in one variable and systems of equations in two variables
stem: If (x, y) is a solution of the system of equations above and x > 0, what is the value of xy?
rationale: From the second equation: 2y + 10 = 2x + 10 + 6x = 8x + 10. So 2y = 8x, i.e., y = 4x. Substituting into y = x2: x2 = 4x. Since x > 0, x = 4 and y = 16. Therefore xy = 64. Choice B is correct.
